model.sql_literal_demo.dq_flags.name: dq_flags
model.sql_literal_demo.dq_flags.raw_code:
{# Synthetic cute-dbt#137 fixture: sql-format givens, one literal-row (tabulates) + one non-literal (falls back). #}
select entity_type, is_valid from {{ ref('src_checks') }}

unit_test.sql_literal_demo.test_dq_flags.name: test_dq_flags_sql_literal_givens

given.rows:
select 'encounters' as entity_type, true as is_valid
union all
select 'medications' as entity_type, false as is_valid
select entity_type, is_valid from src_checks where is_valid

unit_test.sql_literal_demo.test_dq_flags.description: Synthetic cute-dbt#137 unit test. The first given is a LITERAL-ROW sql fixture (`select <literal> as <col> union all ...`) that cute-dbt tabulates as a data table in the Current view, identical to a dict/csv given. The second given is a NON-literal sql fixture (a real FROM/WHERE clause) that cute-dbt conservatively rejects, so it falls back to the syntax-highlighted sql code block. Pure synthetic data only; sql_literal_demo, src_checks, and the rows are fictitious data-quality flags, not real records.
